AI can write code in seconds-but fixing what it writes can cost you days.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Gabriel Heinzer on UnsplashThere was a time when writing 500 lines of code took an entire afternoon.
Today?
An AI assistant can generate those same 500 lines before you’ve finished your coffee.
At first, it feels like magic.
You ship features faster, close more tickets, and feel more productive than ever.
But after working with AI-generated code for months, I noticed something strange.
The more code AI wrote, the harder my projects became to maintain.
Not because AI is bad.
Because most developers are using it the wrong way.
The biggest cost of AI-generated code isn’t your subscription.
It’s the technical debt you don’t notice until months later.
AI Optimizes for “Working,” Not “Maintainable”
Ask an AI to build a feature, and chances are it works.
The API responds.
The UI loads.
The tests might even pass.
